title: 2023 Turmoil
summary:
Main
12 months ago, we titled our closing report of 2022 ‘What the hell just happened?'. 2023 has not seen the same degree of volatility but has still been a turbulent year for the oil and tanker markets, with robust earnings for all sectors owing to strong demand growth, tight supply and ongoing trading inefficiencies caused by geopolitical and climate related events. As the year started, China's decision to lift it's zero covid policy in late 2022 gave oil demand a significant shot in the arm, contributing 77% of this year's 2.3mbd demand growth through an additional 1.78mbd of demand, with the VLCCs feeling much of the benefit.
